task_completion.description: Open the Eagle Nebula H-alpha image in AstroImageJ
task_completion.visual_evidence: The AstroImageJ application is open, displaying an astronomical image, with '656nmos.fits' visible in the image window title bar.

task_completion.description: Read the target Y-coordinate from the instructions file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of 'profile_instructions.txt', showing the target Y-coordinate to the agent.

task_completion.description: Generate an intensity profile across the entire width of the image at the target Y-coordinate
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Profile Plot' window is open in AstroImageJ showing a graph of intensity vs. distance. A horizontal selection line spanning the entire width of the image may be visible on the main image window.

task_completion.description: Export the profile data to a CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog in AstroImageJ saving the plot data, or a file manager/terminal showing 'eagle_ha_profile.csv' has been created in the '~/AstroImages/measurements/' directory.

task_completion.description: Determine peak intensity, peak X-coordinate, mean background, profile length, and FWHM
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is using a spreadsheet, calculator, or terminal (e.g., Python interactive shell or data analysis tool) to process the CSV data, specifically calculating the mean of the lowest 25% and the FWHM.

task_completion.description: Save the summary report with the required labels
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing 'profile_summary.txt' being edited or saved, containing the exact labels: PEAK_INTENSITY, PEAK_POSITION_X, MEAN_BACKGROUND, PROFILE_LENGTH, and FWHM_PIXELS alongside their calculated values.

integrity.description: The agent used AstroImageJ to generate the profile rather than bypassing the GUI with a script
integrity.visual_evidence: The AstroImageJ graphical interface and its native 'Profile Plot' window must be visible in the screenshots. The profile should not be generated solely via a Python/matplotlib script.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely calculated the metrics from the data
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be evidence of the agent opening the CSV, using a spreadsheet, or writing a script/command to compute the mean background and FWHM. The values should not appear magically in the text editor without prior calculation steps.

integrity.description: The agent did not copy-paste precomputed answers or use a single shortcut script to solve the entire task
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory must show a step-by-step process: opening the image, plotting the profile, saving the CSV, calculating metrics, and writing the summary file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses a real NASA/ESA Hubble Space Telescope WFPC2 image of the Eagle Nebula (M16) taken with the F656N H-alpha narrowband filter. The expected output file paths are /home/ga/AstroImages/measurements/eagle_ha_profile.csv and /home/ga/AstroImages/measurements/profile_summary.txt.
